paper_title: Earth System Model COSMOS Simulations for Miocene Climate Analysis

methods.description: Setup of the Earth System Model COSMOS to simulate climate conditions during the Miocene period, focusing on the sensitivity of Arctic Ocean stratification and circulation controlled by FS gateway configurations and CO2 changes.

steps.description: We use the Earth System Model COSMOS, which incorporates the atmosphere model ECHAM5, the ocean model MPI-OM, and the land-vegetation model JSBACH.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize Earth System Model COSMOS with ECHAM5, MPI-OM, and JSBACH components

steps.description: The atmosphere model is used at T31 (~ 3.75° × 3.75°) horizontal resolution with 19 vertical layers.
steps.searchable_summary: configure atmosphere model ECHAM5 with T31 resolution and 19 vertical layers

steps.description: The ocean model has a resolution of GR30 (3° × 1.8°) and has 40 vertical layers.
steps.searchable_summary: configure ocean model MPI-OM with GR30 resolution and 40 vertical layers

steps.description: The interactive exchange of fluxes and energy between ocean and atmosphere is handled via the coupler OASIS3.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate OASIS3 coupler for flux and energy exchange between ocean and atmosphere

steps.description: In our reference simulation (MIO_FW500), we prescribe an atmospheric CO2 concentration of 450 ppm, with an FS width of ~ 500 km and fixed gateway depths of ~ 1500 mbsl.
steps.searchable_summary: set reference simulation parameters with CO2 concentration, FS width, and gateway depth

steps.description: We integrate our model for 4 kyrs to minimize salinity/temperature trends in the deep ocean after initialization with the present-day conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: run model integration for 4 kyrs to stabilize salinity and temperature

methods.description: Conduct sensitivity experiments to analyze the impact of varying FS widths and atmospheric CO2 levels on global ocean circulation and climate during the Miocene period.

steps.description: Sensitivity experiments are performed for the FS by varying its width (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: perform sensitivity experiments by varying FS width

steps.description: Starting from a narrow FS width of ~ 50 km, in a set of six model simulations, we gradually widened the ocean gateway to 105, 222, 286, 352, and 500 km, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct model simulations with FS widths from 50 km to 500 km
inputs: FS widths: 50 km, 105 km, 222 km, 286 km, 352 km, 500 km
outputs: simulation results for each FS width

steps.description: Apart from the FS gateway sensitivity experiments, we performed additional simulations at different levels of atmospheric CO2 (280, 450, 600, and 840 ppm; Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: perform additional simulations with varying CO2 levels
inputs: CO2 levels: 280 ppm, 450 ppm, 600 ppm, 840 ppm
outputs: simulation results for each CO2 level
